title: Run a convergence study to confirm order
description: Halve the step or refine the mesh repeatedly and confirm error shrinks at the method's theoretical rate.
body: Use when verifying that a discretization is implemented correctly. Do not assume a small error means correctness, since a bug can produce small but wrong-order error. Actions: run the simulation at several step sizes, compute error against a reference at each, and fit the observed order of convergence. Validation signals include error decreasing at the expected rate and the fitted order matching the method's theory. Risk boundary: an observed order below theory signals a discretization bug, so escalate to inspecting the scheme rather than just refining further.
